 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","title":"Fix page.tsx: Replace zinc/indigo colors with neutral","description":"# Task: Unify colors in page.tsx

## Context
The main home page (page.tsx) was not updated during the initial UI overhaul, leaving it with the old color scheme while child components use the new palette.

## Specific Changes Required

### Background colors (zinc → neutral)
- Line 133: `bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-950` → `bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-950`
- Line 276: Same pattern in Suspense fallback

### Text colors (zinc → neutral)
- Line 188: `text-zinc-900 dark:text-white` → `text-neutral-900 dark:text-white`
- Line 197: `text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-400` → `text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-400`
- Line 219-232: Footer text colors

### Footer section (lines 215-261)
- Line 215: `dark:border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-900` → `dark:border-neutral-800 bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-900`
- Line 250: `border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-800` → `border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-800`

### Accent colors (indigo → neutral or primary)
- Line 168: "Clear all filters" button - `text-indigo-600 hover:text-indigo-800` → Consider `text-neutral-600 hover:text-neutral-900` or use primary CSS variable
- Line 242: "Try Pro" badge - KEEP indigo (intentional Pro branding)
- Line 256: Twitter link - KEEP indigo (external link accent)

## Design Rationale
- The main page background should match the Hero section (which uses neutral)
- Text colors should be consistent with cards and filters
- "Clear all filters" is a functional link, not a brand element, so it should use neutral
- Pro/external links can retain indigo as intentional accent

## Testing
1. Visual comparison in light mode
2. Visual comparison in dark mode
3. Verify all text remains readable (contrast check)
4. Verify no visual jarring between Hero → Filters → Grid

## Related Files
- This change prepares consistency for FeaturedPromptsSection (child)
- Should match Hero.tsx, CategoryFilter.tsx, TagFilter.tsx patterns","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:42.429379427-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:18:00.265035128-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T02:18:00.265035128-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed all zinc/indigo colors in page.tsx. Replaced with neutral palette. Kept intentional indigo for Pro badge and Twitter link.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:36:37.764755913-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oqw4","title":"E2E Tests: Keyboard Shortcuts","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-avo. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for keyboard shortcuts functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### Global Shortcuts
1. Cmd/Ctrl+K opens command palette
2. Escape closes modals/overlays
3. / focuses search
4. ? shows shortcuts help
5. G then H goes home

### Navigation Shortcuts
6. G then L goes to library
7. G then S goes to search
8. G then P goes to profile
9. G then B goes to billing

### Content Shortcuts
10. N creates new prompt
11. E edits current prompt
12. C copies prompt content
13. S saves to library
14. D deletes (with confirm)

### Selection Shortcuts
15. J/K moves up/down in lists
16. X toggles selection
17. Shift+click multi-select
18. Cmd/Ctrl+A select all

### Shortcut Help
19. Help modal shows all shortcuts
20. Shortcuts grouped logically
21. Searchable shortcuts list

## Files to Create
- e2e/shortcuts/global.spec.ts
- e2e/shortcuts/navigation.spec.ts
- e2e/shortcuts/content.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/shortcuts-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- All shortcuts work reliably
- No conflicts with browser shortcuts
- Help accessible everywhere

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oyg6","title":"Implement public status page and incident management","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cr0q.

## Task
Create a public status page showing service health and incidents.

## Why This Matters
- Transparency with users
- Reduce support tickets
- Professional SaaS expectation
- Incident communication

## Features

### 1. Status Page
- Overall system status
- Individual component status
- Uptime percentage (30/90 days)
- Response time metrics

### 2. Components to Monitor
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Web App | Main application |
| API | REST API endpoints |
| Authentication | Login/signup |
| Database | Data operations |
| Search | Search functionality |
| Payments | Stripe integration |
| Email | Transactional emails |

### 3. Status Levels
- Operational (green)
- Degraded Performance (yellow)
- Partial Outage (orange)
- Major Outage (red)
- Maintenance (blue)

### 4. Incident Management
- Create incident
- Update incident status
- Post-incident report
- Incident history
- Subscribe to updates

### 5. Scheduled Maintenance
- Announce maintenance windows
- Countdown to maintenance
- Automatic status change
- Completion notification

## Implementation

### Status Check
```typescript
interface ComponentStatus {
  name: string;
  status: 'operational' | 'degraded' | 'partial' | 'major' | 'maintenance';
  latency?: number;
  lastChecked: Date;
}

async function checkComponentHealth(component: string): Promise<ComponentStatus> {
  switch (component) {
    case 'database':
      return await checkDatabaseHealth();
    case 'api':
      return await checkAPIHealth();
    // ... other components
  }
}
```

### Database Schema
```typescript
export const incidents = pgTable('incidents', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  title: text('title').notNull(),
  status: text('status').notNull(), // investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved
  impact: text('impact').notNull(), // none, minor, major, critical
  affectedComponents: text('affected_components').array(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at'),
});

export const incidentUpdates = pgTable('incident_updates', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  incidentId: uuid('incident_id').references(() => incidents.id).notNull(),
  status: text('status').notNull(),
  message: text('message').notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const statusSubscribers = pgTable('status_subscribers', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  email: text('email').notNull().unique(),
  confirmedAt: timestamp('confirmed_at'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## Public Pages
- /status - Main status page
- /status/history - Incident history
- /status/subscribe - Email subscription

## Admin Pages
- /admin/status - Manage status
- /admin/incidents - Manage incidents
- /admin/maintenance - Schedule maintenance

## Integration
- Integrate with uptime monitoring (nelm)
- Automatic incident creation on outage
- Webhook to Slack/Discord

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/status/page.tsx
- src/app/api/status/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/admin/status/page.tsx
- src/lib/status/health-checks.ts
- src/lib/status/incidents.ts

## Success Criteria
- Status page always accessible
- Real-time status updates
- Incident communication clear
- Subscription system works

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-p8b6","title":"E2E Tests: Public Status Page","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2qj5.

## Task
Create E2E tests for the public status page.

## Test Scenarios
1. **Status Page Display**
   - Page loads correctly
   - Current status shows
   - All services listed
   - Uptime percentages display

2. **Incident History**
   - Past incidents shown
   - Incident timeline works
   - Status updates visible
   - Resolution time displayed

3. **Subscribe to Updates**
   - Email subscription works
   - Webhook subscription works
   - Unsubscribe works

4. **Admin Incident Management**
   - Create incident (admin)
   - Update incident status
   - Resolve incident
   - Post-mortem publishing

## Logging Requirements
- Log page views
- Log subscription events
- Log incident state changes
- Screenshot status states

## Files to Create
- e2e/status/page.spec.ts
- e2e/status/subscribe.spec.ts
- e2e/admin/incidents.spec.ts

## Success Criteria
- Status page accurate
- Real-time updates work
- Subscription reliable
- Admin tools functional
